headline: Nurse-to-Patient Ratio at Public Hospitals Reaches 1 to 12
context: Government hospitals report nurse staffing ratios far below international standards amid recruitment challenges.

cards.big: Public hospital nurse-to-patient ratio of 1 to 12 double the WHO recommended 1 to 6
cards.sub: Night shifts often see ratios worsen to 1 nurse for 20 patients

cards.big: Malaysia has 4.8 nurses per 1,000 population versus OECD average of 8.8
cards.sub: Nursing workforce gap of 42,000 persists despite annual training of 8,000 new nurses

cards.big: Nurse burnout survey shows 62% report emotional exhaustion scores above clinical threshold
cards.sub: Understaffing creates a vicious cycle as exhausted nurses leave accelerating shortages

cards.big: Singapore retained nurses by raising starting salaries 30% and creating career ladders in 2023
cards.sub: Competitive compensation combined with professional development reduced attrition

cards.big: Overworked nurses make more errors—understaffing is a patient safety crisis not just a labor issue
Should Malaysia legislate maximum nurse-to-patient ratios in all public hospitals
